title: Answer To The Following Question Of Mrs Howe.
content:
What is prudery?

    'Tis a bledam,
    Seen with wit and beauty seldom.
    'Tis a fear that starts at shadows.
    Tis, (no, 'tisn't) like Miss Meadows.
    'Tis a virgin hard of feature,
    Old, and void of all good-nature;
    Lean and fretful; would seem wise;
    Yet plays the fool before she dies.
    'Tis an ugly, envious shrew,
    That rails at dear Lepell and you.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Alexander Pope, titled "Answer To The Following Question Of Mrs Howe.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
